canonical_definition: Synthetic Complexity Score, a quantitative measure from 1 to 5 that assesses a molecule's complexity while considering its synthetic accessibility, used to guide pathway selection.
papers: OSDA Agent: Leveraging Large Language Models for De Novo Design of Organic Structure Directing Agents
RetroInText: A Multimodal Large Language Model Enhanced Framework for Retrosynthetic Planning via In-Context Representation Learning

definitions.text: Synthetic Complexity Score, a quantitative measure from 1 to 5 that assesses a molecule's complexity while considering its synthetic accessibility, used to guide pathway selection.
definitions.paper: RetroInText: A Multimodal Large Language Model Enhanced Framework for Retrosynthetic Planning via In-Context Representation Learning

definitions.text: A data-driven scoring method, trained on the Reaxys reaction database, that predicts the synthetic complexity of a molecule, with lower scores indicating easier synthesis.
definitions.paper: OSDA Agent: Leveraging Large Language Models for De Novo Design of Organic Structure Directing Agents

relationships.paper: OSDA Agent: Leveraging Large Language Models for De Novo Design of Organic Structure Directing Agents
relationships.evidence: The results indicate that our OSDA Agent successfully generated optimized versions of these molecules, reducing the SCScore and thereby lowering the synthesis difficulty, while preserving the functional characteristics necessary for zeolite templating. (Section 5.3)
